mnemonics: Prior Belief + New Data = Updated Belief (P + D = U)
Bayes' Rule: Posterior is Proportional to Likelihood times Prior (PPLP)
keyPoints: Bayes' Theorem updates prior beliefs with observed data to form a posterior distribution.
The posterior distribution is the complete solution to the estimation problem, containing all information about the parameters.
Estimation often involves computationally intensive methods like Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) for complex models.
commonMistakes: Confusing the prior with the posterior, treating the initial belief as the result instead of the starting point.
Ignoring the influence of the chosen prior, especially with limited data, which can bias the results.
Assuming the posterior mean is always the best point estimate, without considering the loss function and the full posterior distribution.
